text:
The `-c` flag resumed our existing session history (1,216 messages!), so the turns include messages from our entire conversation. But the key turns are visible:

- **Turn 2** (iter 2-4): "What are the main crates" — 1 agent + 4 subagent iterations
- **Turn 4** (iter 11-14): "Most complex code" — 4 parent iterations with Bash/Read
- **Turn 5** (iter 15-16): "Suggest refactoring" — 2 parent iterations, Read + text response

The message count jumping between 1 msg and 1,216+ msgs shows iteration #001 and #005 loaded the full session history, while #002-#004 were subagent calls (fresh session, 1-5 msgs).

tool_uses.name: Bash
tool_uses.input:
{"command": "git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
add multi-turn capture (3 user messages, session continuity)

20 API calls across 3 turns using --continue flag
Shows: session accumulation, turn boundaries, context growth between turns
Turn detection added to build_viz_data.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3", "description": "Commit and push", "timeout": 120000}
